differential_diagnoses: Autoimmune hemolytic anemia (AIHA) with potential underlying lymphoproliferative disorder
next_steps: Flow cytometry of peripheral blood for PNH (CD55 and CD59 expression)
Bone marrow aspiration and biopsy with cytogenetics and flow cytometry
Serum levels of haptoglobin, free hemoglobin, and ferritin
Serum soluble CD25 and triglyceride levels to screen for HLH
Computed tomography (CT) of chest, abdomen, and pelvis
Direct antiglobulin test (Coombs test) including specific subclass testing

differential_diagnoses: Autoimmune hemolytic anemia (Cold agglutinin disease or mixed-type)
Bone marrow failure syndrome (e.g., Fanconi anemia)
next_steps: Perform flow cytometry for PNH (CD55 and CD59 analysis on neutrophils and RBCs)
Order serum B12, folate, copper, and ceruloplasmin levels
Order haptoglobin, LDH, reticulocyte count, and repeat DAT with cold agglutinin screening
Perform bone marrow aspiration and biopsy with cytogenetics and molecular testing for bone marrow failure syndromes
Check serum ferritin, soluble CD25, and triglycerides to evaluate for HLH
Perform targeted physical examination and clinical genetic counseling for signs of Fanconi anemia
Review peripheral blood smear with a hematopathologist for morphologic evidence of hemolysis or dysplasia
